summary: The compiletest assembly-llvm test for `tests/assembly-llvm/wasm_exceptions.rs` failed when targeting `wasm32-wasip1`. A filecheck directive was not satisfied in the generated assembly output, indicated by the `>>>>>>` marker after line 133 of the assembly. This caused the bootstrap build to fail.
root_cause: The filecheck CHECK directives in `tests/assembly-llvm/wasm_exceptions.rs` were not satisfied by the actual assembly output generated for the `wasm32-wasip1` target. The filecheck run terminated with `>>>>>>` after matching through line 133 of the assembly, indicating a subsequent expected pattern was not found in the output.

evidence.reason: Explicitly names the failing test in the compiletest assembly-llvm suite.
The filecheck `>>>>>>` marker indicates the point where a CHECK directive was not matched in the actual assembly output, confirming a pattern mismatch.

evidence.quote: test result: FAILED. 550 passed; 1 failed; 103 ignored; 0 measured; 0 filtered out; finished in 15.38s
evidence.reason: Confirms exactly one test failed in the assembly-llvm suite.

evidence.quote: Some tests failed in compiletest suite=assembly-llvm mode=assembly host=x86_64-unknown-linux-gnu target=wasm32-wasip1
evidence.reason: Identifies the compiletest suite, mode, and target (wasm32-wasip1) where the failure occurred.

evidence.quote: Bootstrap failed while executing `--stage 2 test --host= --target wasm32-wasip1 tests/run-make tests/run-make-cargo tests/ui tests/mir-opt tests/codegen-units tests/codegen-llvm tests/assembly-llvm library/core`
evidence.reason: Confirms the overall build failure was triggered by the assembly-llvm test failure on the wasm32-wasip1 target.

suggested_fix: Update the filecheck CHECK directives in `tests/assembly-llvm/wasm_exceptions.rs` to match the current assembly output generated for `wasm32-wasip1`, or fix the code change that caused the assembly output to diverge from the existing expected patterns.

model_info._backfill_note: Pre-Codex-2026-05-14 F2 fix. The Claude shim now persists _model_info from every API call; this row predates that emit so resolved_model and usage are not recoverable. requested_model is set to the shim-level alias (haiku for v1, sonnet for v2) which is what the post-fix shim emits.
